name: Check Cashing Etc.
description_short: A1 Check Cashing is a Detroit-based check cashing service offering same-day payouts for payroll, settlement, tax refund, and large checks with competitive fees.
description_long:
A1 Check Cashing is a locally-owned check cashing business operating in Detroit, Michigan since at least the mid-1990s (claiming 30 years of experience). The company is located at 19131 Telegraph Road in Detroit and serves Wayne County, Macomb County, and surrounding areas including Dearborn, Southfield, Westland, Livonia, and Highland Park. The business positions itself as a same-day financial services provider for individuals who need immediate access to funds from various check types.

A1 Check Cashing offers a comprehensive range of check cashing services including payroll checks (ADP, Paycheck, Paylocity platforms), 401(k) distributions (Fidelity, Voya Financial, Wells Fargo), lawsuit settlement checks, title company checks, income tax checks, tax refund checks, business checks, workers' compensation checks, and cashier's checks. The company also provides ancillary services such as debit card withdrawals, document notarization, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment. Their stated value proposition centers on same-day payouts with no holds and no hidden fees, positioning themselves as offering Detroit's lowest check cashing fees.

A1 differentiates itself through claimed operational features including 30 years of local experience, private transaction rooms, tinted windows, state-of-the-art security technology, and location in a well-protected neighborhood. Customer testimonials emphasize polite service, competitive pricing on large checks, and family-like treatment. The company operates Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. and encourages customers to call (313) 255-9400 for free fee quotes before cashing checks.

As a traditional check cashing service, A1 Cashing Etc. serves consumers who lack bank accounts or prefer immediate cash access over standard banking deposits. However, no specific fee schedule is published on the website, and actual costs depend on check type and amount. The service is transactional rather than relationship-based, and customers should verify current fee structures before using the service. Limited online presence and no digital/mobile cashing options suggest the business is primarily walk-in oriented.
pros: Same-day payouts with no holds on checks
Claims Detroit's lowest check cashing fees with free quote service
Accepts 10+ check types including 401(k)s, settlements, tax refunds, and business checks
30 years of local operating experience in Detroit market
Private transaction rooms with tinted windows and state-of-the-art security
Extended service hours (9 a.m. to 6 p.m. weekdays) with weekend closure accommodated by phone
Positive customer testimonials highlighting low fees on large checks and courteous service
cons: No published fee schedule on website—must call or visit for pricing quotes
Limited to one physical location (19131 Telegraph Rd) despite serving multiple counties
No online or mobile check cashing options; requires in-person visits during business hours
Closed weekends and evenings, limiting access for working customers
Limited digital footprint and no apparent account history or verification features online
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked Detroit residents needing immediate cash from payroll or settlement checks
Business owners requiring same-day access to corporate check funds
Individuals cashing large checks who want competitive fee quotes before committing
diagnosis: A1 Check Cashing is best suited for Detroit-area residents and business owners who need same-day access to cash and prefer local, face-to-face service over traditional banking. The main caveat is that actual fee structures are not transparent on the website—callers must request quotes for specific check amounts and types, making cost comparison difficult before visiting.
services: Payroll check cashing (ADP, Paycheck, Paylocity compatible)
401(k) check cashing (Fidelity, Voya Financial, Wells Fargo compatible)
Money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment (referenced but not detailed)
